visa_types.name: Conference/ Seminar Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of with two blank pages)
- Two recent passport size photographs
- Copy of profile page of passport. If applicant is holding dual nationality, copy of profile page of both passports.
- National address printout
- To and fro confirmed tickets
- Invitation letter from sponsor to attend the conference/ seminar
- Political and Security clearance of the event issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs and the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), Government of India
- Applicable Visa Fee
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. [Table:    Business Visa]
Visa fee is processing charges. It cannot be refunded under any circumstances, whether Visa is denied or granted for a shorter period than applied for. Once Visa is granted, it cannot be changed or amended.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business visa is granted to a foreigner for the following purposes –
- Foreign nationals who wish to visit India to establish an industrial/ business venture or to explore possibilities to set up an industrial/business venture, other than Proprietorship Firms and Partnership Firms, in India.
- Foreign nationals coming to India to purchase/sell industrial products or commercial products or consumer durables.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for technical meetings/discussions, attending Board meetings or general meetings for providing business services support.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for recruitment of manpower.
- Foreign nationals who are partners in the business and/or functioning as Directors of the company.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for consultations regarding exhibitions or for participation in exhibitions, trade fairs, business fairs etc.
- Foreign buyers who come to transact business with suppliers/ potential suppliers at locations in India, to evaluate or monitor quality, give specifications, place orders, negotiate further supplies etc., relating to goods or services procured from India.
- Foreign experts/specialists on a visit of short duration in connection with an ongoing project with the objective of monitoring the progress of the work, conducting meetings with Indian customers and/or to provide technical guidance.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for pre-sales or post-sales activity not amounting to actual execution of any contract or project.
- Foreign trainees of multinational companies/corporate houses coming for in-house training in the regional hubs of the concerned company located in India.
- Foreign nationals coming as tour conductors and travel agents and / or conducting business tours of foreigners or business relating to it, etc.
- Foreign academicians/ experts coming under GIAN (Global Initiative for Academic Networks).
- Crew members of scheduled/ non-scheduled flights operated by scheduled airlines, nonscheduled and chartered flights operated by non-scheduled airlines and special flights.
- Foreign nationals intending to visit India to participate in cultural events/ activities with remuneration. [Such foreign nationals intending to visit India to participate in cultural events/ activities for short duration without remuneration may be granted Entry (X-Misc.) Visa.]
- Foreign nationals who are engaged in commercial sports events in India on contract (including coaches) like Indian Premier League, Indian Soccer League, etc. with remuneration. They may be granted ‘B-Sports’ Visa with multiple entry facility for appropriate period. Such a foreign national shall comply with all the statutory obligations like payment of taxes, etc.
Note : 
- The foreign national should not be visiting India for the business of money lending or for running a petty business or petty trade or for full time employment in India, etc.
- The foreign national shall comply with all other requirements like payment of tax liabilities etc.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business visa is granted to a foreigner for the following purposes –
- Foreign nationals who wish to visit India to establish an industrial/ business venture or to explore possibilities to set up an industrial/business venture, other than Proprietorship Firms and Partnership Firms, in India.
- Foreign nationals coming to India to purchase/sell industrial products or commercial products or consumer durables.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for technical meetings/discussions, attending Board meetings or general meetings for providing business services support.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for recruitment of manpower.
- Foreign nationals who are partners in the business and/or functioning as Directors of the company.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for consultations regarding exhibitions or for participation in exhibitions, trade fairs, business fairs etc.
- Foreign buyers who come to transact business with suppliers/ potential suppliers at locations in India, to evaluate or monitor quality, give specifications, place orders, negotiate further supplies etc., relating to goods or services procured from India.
- Foreign experts/specialists on a visit of short duration in connection with an ongoing project with the objective of monitoring the progress of the work, conducting meetings with Indian customers and/or to provide technical guidance.
- Foreign nationals coming to India for pre-sales or post-sales activity not amounting to actual execution of any contract or project.
- Foreign trainees of multinational companies/corporate houses coming for in-house training in the regional hubs of the concerned company located in India.
- Foreign nationals coming as tour conductors and travel agents and / or conducting business tours of foreigners or business relating to it, etc.
- Foreign academicians/ experts coming under GIAN (Global Initiative for Academic Networks).
- Crew members of scheduled/ non-scheduled flights operated by scheduled airlines, nonscheduled and chartered flights operated by non-scheduled airlines and special flights.
- Foreign nationals intending to visit India to participate in cultural events/ activities with remuneration. [Such foreign nationals intending to visit India to participate in cultural events/ activities for short duration without remuneration may be granted Entry (X-Misc.) Visa.]
- Foreign nationals who are engaged in commercial sports events in India on contract (including coaches) like Indian Premier League, Indian Soccer League, etc. with remuneration. They may be granted ‘B-Sports’ Visa with multiple entry facility for appropriate period. Such a foreign national shall comply with all the statutory obligations like payment of taxes, etc.
Note : 
- The foreign national should not be visiting India for the business of money lending or for running a petty business or petty trade or for full time employment in India, etc.
- The foreign national shall comply with all other requirements like payment of tax liabilities etc.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).
Business visa fee for Iraqi nationals: 

[Table:    Business Visa]
Visa fee is processing charges. It cannot be refunded under any circumstances, whether Visa is denied or granted for a shorter period than applied for. Once Visa is granted, it cannot be changed or amended.

## Documents Required
- A letter of invitation from business establishment in India
- Registration certificate of Indian firm/company (certificate of incorporation)
- Registration letter of Iraqi company (from local chamber of commerce)
- Proof of financial standing (latest Bank Statement)
- Duly completed Online Application Form
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Copy of Passport
- Two recent Passport size photographs
- Applicable Visa Fee
- National Identity card of the applicant (proof of residence)

## Photo Specifications

[Table:    Business Visa]

## Processing Time
Business Visa – 1 Month or more.

## Download Form
Visa Online Application:
                              https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianvisareg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/)

visa_types.name: Visa For Pakistan Nationals / Pakistan Origin Foreign Nationals
visa_types.documents_required:
- Two recent Passport size photographs.
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages).
- Foreign Nationals of Pakistan origin have to apply visa on Pakistani passport only
- Copy of profile page of passport. If applicant is holding dual nationality, copy of profile page of both passports.
- Applicable Visa fee
- All applications of Pakistan nationals and Pakistan Origin are accepted after prior approval from the Embassy.
- Pakistan nationals are issued visas to see their close blood relatives, Medical treatment and business purposes.
- Pakistan nationals visiting close relatives, for business, and Medical Visa, same conditions as in the case of other nationals (but visa applied on Pakistani Visa form in quadruplet) have to submit 4 visa applications
- Affidavit from close relative in India.
- SPONSORSHIP CERTIFICATE is required for Visitor Visa by Pakistani Nationals
- ADDITIONAL FORM to be submitted by Pakistani Nationals and Persons of Pakistani origin
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Two recent passport size photographs
- Applicable visa fee
- Copy of profile page of passport. If applicant is holding dual nationality, copy of profile page of both passports.
- To and fro confirmed tickets
- Declaration in respect of photography equipment being carried, if any.
- Declaration in respect of engaging an Indian liaison officer (wherever applicable).
- Journalists, editors, writers, publishers, etc. who are visiting India for purposes like tourism are requested to provide an undertaking that they would not engage in any journalistic or reporting activities during/after their trip

The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to visit India for journalism purposes.
Journalist Visa is issued to professional journalists, photographers, film producers/directors (other than commercial films), representatives of radio or television channels, travel writers/photographers, etc. travelling to India. A correspondent, columnist, cartoonist, editor or professional journalist working for an association/company engaged in production or broadcast of audio news or audio visual news or current affairs programme through print media, electronic or any other form should apply for Journalist Visa for travel to India. Journalists visiting India for any other purpose like tourism should also apply for Journalist Visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to visit India for journalism purposes.
Journalist Visa is issued to professional journalists, photographers, film producers/directors (other than commercial films), representatives of radio or television channels, travel writers/photographers, etc. travelling to India. A correspondent, columnist, cartoonist, editor or professional journalist working for an association/company engaged in production or broadcast of audio news or audio visual news or current affairs programme through print media, electronic or any other form should apply for Journalist Visa for travel to India. Journalists visiting India for any other purpose like tourism should also apply for Journalist Visa.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Two recent Passport size photographs.
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Copy of profile page of passport. If applicant is holding dual nationality, copy of profile page of both passports.
- Applicable Visa fee
- Copy of Contract showing terms and conditions, including nature of job, details of emoluments,  tax related issues signed by both employer and prospective employee
- A letter from company/ sponsor reg. requirement like payment of tax liabilities
- Documentary proof of educational qualifications and professional expertise
- Applicant to be present in person
- Copy of Iraqi national ID.  Non Iraqi passport holders need to provide a copy of their recent Iraq visa/visa extension page.
- Proof of availability of funds/bank statement for the duration of stay in India.
- To and fro confirmed tickets
- Applicants who are not resident in Iraq are required to fill in the PERSONAL PARTICULARS FORM available at the end of these instructions in addition to the Visa application form.
- Offer of appointment from the applicant’s company clearly mentioning the profile of the company and complete details of the profile & passport of the applicant.
- Letter from Indian Company stating why they are unable to recruit the post from India.
- Copies of documents relating to company's incorporation in India
- Recommendation from Iraqi company in case of transfer/secondment.
- Resume/C.V showing qualification and work experience of the applicant.
Employment Visa holders are required to register with the Foreigners Regional Registration Officer (FRRO) within 14 days of arrival in India.
Entry Visa may be issued to the applicant's dependent family members on production of proof of their relationship (i.e. marriage certificate in case of spouse and birth certificate in case ofchildren).
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

[Table:    Employment Visa]

visa_types.processing_time: Employment Visa – 1 Month or more, some can take longer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to visit employment or project purposes.
Employment visa is issued to Executive Posts and highly skilled and qualified professionals who are taking up assignment/employment with reputed Indian companies or parent/subsidiary companies registered in India. An authenticated copy of the Employment contract and proof of registration of the employing organization are to be furnished. Employment visa is granted for the duration of the employment contract up to two years.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to visit employment or project purposes.
Employment visa is issued to Executive Posts and highly skilled and qualified professionals who are taking up assignment/employment with reputed Indian companies or parent/subsidiary companies registered in India. An authenticated copy of the Employment contract and proof of registration of the employing organization are to be furnished. Employment visa is granted for the duration of the employment contract up to two years.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.

- Copy of Contract showing terms and conditions, including nature of job, details of emoluments,  tax related issues signed by both employer and prospective employee
- A letter from company/ sponsor reg. requirement like payment of tax liabilities
- Documentary proof of educational qualifications and professional expertise
- Applicant to be present in person
- Copy of Iraqi national ID.  Non Iraqi passport holders need to provide a copy of their recent Iraq visa/visa extension page.
- Proof of availability of funds/bank statement for the duration of stay in India.
- To and fro confirmed tickets
- Applicants who are not resident in Iraq are required to fill in the PERSONAL PARTICULARS FORM available at the end of these instructions in addition to the Visa application form.
- Offer of appointment from the applicant’s company clearly mentioning the profile of the company and complete details of the profile & passport of the applicant.
- Letter from Indian Company stating why they are unable to recruit the post from India.
- Copies of documents relating to company's incorporation in India
- Recommendation from Iraqi company in case of transfer/secondment.
- Resume/C.V showing qualification and work experience of the applicant.
Employment Visa holders are required to register with the Foreigners Regional Registration Officer (FRRO) within 14 days of arrival in India.
Entry Visa may be issued to the applicant's dependent family members on production of proof of their relationship (i.e. marriage certificate in case of spouse and birth certificate in case ofchildren).

visa_types.name: Medical Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Medical visits to India: Advisory (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ZmTixIedsawFCMKf3MUj4/aae18432a259a49d4fa3440c81d00fd3/advisory.pdf)
- It is mandatory to obtain a medical visa for the patient and his/her companion through the office licensed by the Indian Embassy in Baghdad or the Consulate General of India in Erbil for the Kurdistan Region, as it is the legal visa that allows its holder to receive treatment in India.
- The medical visa can be extended by the Department of Immigration of India provided that there are reasons for continuing treatment from the approved hospitals.
- The Indian tourist visa is not extendable under any circumstances and it is necessary to leave before the expiry of its validity period in order to avoid travel bans and financial penalties.
- It is preferable to communicate directly with accredited hospitals and specialized doctors through their websites or visit them directly without an intermediary. Hospitals and doctors must also be assured not to include any intermediary in their records and send recent medical reports of the patient in English directly to the hospital for review.
- Make sure to obtain an official letter and an invitation from the hospital stating its consent to receive the patient, an initial determination of the treatment plan, expected duration, and estimated costs, and to include that invitation for the procedures for obtaining a medical visa.
- Beware of dealing with intermediaries such as medical coordination offices that are not licensed by the Indian Ministry of Health, taxi owners, translators, medical treatment brokers and others, in order to avoid those parties getting a financial commission of the amount of the treatment bill.
- Beware of people who promote hospitals, clinics and medical clinics through social media and internet sites.
- Do not transfer money to intermediaries and unknown persons.
- Ensure that the prescription is carried upon departure and check the expiration date of the medicines.
The Indian Embassy wishes safety and recovery for all, stressing the importance and need to adhere to the above-mentioned guidelines.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A medical visa (MED Visa) is granted to a foreigner whose sole purpose is to seek medical treatment in established/recognized/specialized hospitals/treatment centers in India. In case the foreign national desires to avail of treatment under the Indian systems of medicine, his/her case will also be considered. 
Hospital Authorities must share the documents issued by them e.g. medical invitation letter, appointment letter etc. as per the format given in Appendix I on the official email ID of concerned Indian Mission/ Post.
The patient coming to India for medical treatment can be permitted one or two attendants, subject to hospital recommendation and they can be granted medical attendant visa (Med X) visa co-terminus with the ‘Med visa’ of the patient.
Medical visits to India: Advisory (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ZmTixIedsawFCMKf3MUj4/aae18432a259a49d4fa3440c81d00fd3/advisory.pdf)
- It is mandatory to obtain a medical visa for the patient and his/her companion through the office licensed by the Indian Embassy in Baghdad or the Consulate General of India in Erbil for the Kurdistan Region, as it is the legal visa that allows its holder to receive treatment in India.
- The medical visa can be extended by the Department of Immigration of India provided that there are reasons for continuing treatment from the approved hospitals.
- The Indian tourist visa is not extendable under any circumstances and it is necessary to leave before the expiry of its validity period in order to avoid travel bans and financial penalties.
- It is preferable to communicate directly with accredited hospitals and specialized doctors through their websites or visit them directly without an intermediary. Hospitals and doctors must also be assured not to include any intermediary in their records and send recent medical reports of the patient in English directly to the hospital for review.
- Make sure to obtain an official letter and an invitation from the hospital stating its consent to receive the patient, an initial determination of the treatment plan, expected duration, and estimated costs, and to include that invitation for the procedures for obtaining a medical visa.
- Beware of dealing with intermediaries such as medical coordination offices that are not licensed by the Indian Ministry of Health, taxi owners, translators, medical treatment brokers and others, in order to avoid those parties getting a financial commission of the amount of the treatment bill.
- Beware of people who promote hospitals, clinics and medical clinics through social media and internet sites.
- Do not transfer money to intermediaries and unknown persons.
- Ensure that the prescription is carried upon departure and check the expiration date of the medicines.
The Indian Embassy wishes safety and recovery for all, stressing the importance and need to adhere to the above-mentioned guidelines.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A medical visa (MED Visa) is granted to a foreigner whose sole purpose is to seek medical treatment in established/recognized/specialized hospitals/treatment centers in India. In case the foreign national desires to avail of treatment under the Indian systems of medicine, his/her case will also be considered. 
Hospital Authorities must share the documents issued by them e.g. medical invitation letter, appointment letter etc. as per the format given in Appendix I on the official email ID of concerned Indian Mission/ Post.
The patient coming to India for medical treatment can be permitted one or two attendants, subject to hospital recommendation and they can be granted medical attendant visa (Med X) visa co-terminus with the ‘Med visa’ of the patient.
Medical visits to India: Advisory (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ZmTixIedsawFCMKf3MUj4/aae18432a259a49d4fa3440c81d00fd3/advisory.pdf)
- It is mandatory to obtain a medical visa for the patient and his/her companion through the office licensed by the Indian Embassy in Baghdad or the Consulate General of India in Erbil for the Kurdistan Region, as it is the legal visa that allows its holder to receive treatment in India.
- The medical visa can be extended by the Department of Immigration of India provided that there are reasons for continuing treatment from the approved hospitals.
- The Indian tourist visa is not extendable under any circumstances and it is necessary to leave before the expiry of its validity period in order to avoid travel bans and financial penalties.
- It is preferable to communicate directly with accredited hospitals and specialized doctors through their websites or visit them directly without an intermediary. Hospitals and doctors must also be assured not to include any intermediary in their records and send recent medical reports of the patient in English directly to the hospital for review.
- Make sure to obtain an official letter and an invitation from the hospital stating its consent to receive the patient, an initial determination of the treatment plan, expected duration, and estimated costs, and to include that invitation for the procedures for obtaining a medical visa.
- Beware of dealing with intermediaries such as medical coordination offices that are not licensed by the Indian Ministry of Health, taxi owners, translators, medical treatment brokers and others, in order to avoid those parties getting a financial commission of the amount of the treatment bill.
- Beware of people who promote hospitals, clinics and medical clinics through social media and internet sites.
- Do not transfer money to intermediaries and unknown persons.
- Ensure that the prescription is carried upon departure and check the expiration date of the medicines.
The Indian Embassy wishes safety and recovery for all, stressing the importance and need to adhere to the above-mentioned guidelines.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

[Table:    Medical Visa]
Visa fee is processing charges. It cannot be refunded under any circumstances, whether Visa is denied or granted for a shorter period than applied for. Once Visa is granted, it cannot be changed or amended.

## Documents Required
Medical visits to India: Advisory (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ZmTixIedsawFCMKf3MUj4/aae18432a259a49d4fa3440c81d00fd3/advisory.pdf)
- It is mandatory to obtain a medical visa for the patient and his/her companion through the office licensed by the Indian Embassy in Baghdad or the Consulate General of India in Erbil for the Kurdistan Region, as it is the legal visa that allows its holder to receive treatment in India.
- The medical visa can be extended by the Department of Immigration of India provided that there are reasons for continuing treatment from the approved hospitals.
- The Indian tourist visa is not extendable under any circumstances and it is necessary to leave before the expiry of its validity period in order to avoid travel bans and financial penalties.
- It is preferable to communicate directly with accredited hospitals and specialized doctors through their websites or visit them directly without an intermediary. Hospitals and doctors must also be assured not to include any intermediary in their records and send recent medical reports of the patient in English directly to the hospital for review.
- Make sure to obtain an official letter and an invitation from the hospital stating its consent to receive the patient, an initial determination of the treatment plan, expected duration, and estimated costs, and to include that invitation for the procedures for obtaining a medical visa.
- Beware of dealing with intermediaries such as medical coordination offices that are not licensed by the Indian Ministry of Health, taxi owners, translators, medical treatment brokers and others, in order to avoid those parties getting a financial commission of the amount of the treatment bill.
- Beware of people who promote hospitals, clinics and medical clinics through social media and internet sites.
- Do not transfer money to intermediaries and unknown persons.
- Ensure that the prescription is carried upon departure and check the expiration date of the medicines.
The Indian Embassy wishes safety and recovery for all, stressing the importance and need to adhere to the above-mentioned guidelines.

visa_types.name: Entry Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Two recent passport size photographs
- Applicable visa fee
- Copy of Passport
- Registration/Naturalization certificate of current nationality002E
- To and fro confirmed tickets
- Letter of employment
- Copy of the previous expired/canceled Indian Passport and Surrender Certificate or Deemed Surrender Certificate. If the applicant had never held Indian passport, copy of last held Indian passport and surrender/deemed surrender certificate of parents or OCI/PIO card of parents along with birth certificate of applicant is required.
- Spouses of Indian national or of Indian origin, legalized/ apostilled marriage certificate is required
- In case of minor having foreign passport, copies of both parents are required.
- Foreigners who are married to Indian Nationals are required to submit copy of Indian Passport of spouse along with a copy of marriage certificate
- Valid exit - entry copy for non-Iraqi
Indians who have acquired foreign nationality must surrender their Indian passports and obtain  Surrender Certificate/Deemed Surrender Certificate from the Passport Section of Embassy of India, Baghdad
IN CASE OF ENTRY VISA FOR SPORTS PERSONS
- Invitation from Concerned Sports Association/Authority in India
- Recommedation letter from Concerned Sports Association in Iraq
- Approval from the Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports of Government of India.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
An Entry (‘X’) Visa can be granted to a foreigner in the following cases :-
- A Person of Indian Origin, who do not possess an OCI card, and may be granted ‘X-1’
- Spouse and children of an Indian citizen/ Person of Indian Origin/ OCI cardholder (other than those who are registered as OCI cardholder) may be granted ‘X-2’
- Foreign nationals coming to join Auroville Foundation shall be issued X-3 visa.
- Foreign nationals coming to join the Missionaries of Charity, Kolkata may be granted multiple entry ‘X-3’ Visa.
- Foreign nationals who own property in India may be granted multiple entry ‘X-4’ Visa for a period of one year. The foreign national must submit documentary proof of permission of the Reserve Bank of India for the purchase of / holding the property in India and the document should show registration of the particular property with the registration authority concerned. The one year multi entry ‘X-4’ visa can be extended by the FRRO/FRO concerned on yearly basis up to a maximum period of 5 years from the date of initial visa.
- Diplomats/ Officials on a personal (Non-official) visit to India may be granted ‘X-5’ Visa for appropriate duration taking into account the purpose of visit.
- Ordinary passport holding dependents of diplomats/ officials assigned to India (including in the UN and Non-UN International Organizations) may be granted ‘X-5D’ Visa for appropriate duration.
- Participants in international Sports events and the officials (including coach) of the Sports teams (other than those who are engaged in commercial sports events in India on contract like Indian Premier League, Indian Soccer League, Indian Badminton League etc. with remuneration) may be granted ‘X-SP’ Visa of appropriate duration.
- Foreign nationals applying for visa for a purpose, which is not specifically covered under any visa category, may be granted ‘X-Misc’ Visa for appropriate duration.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
An Entry (‘X’) Visa can be granted to a foreigner in the following cases :-
- A Person of Indian Origin, who do not possess an OCI card, and may be granted ‘X-1’
- Spouse and children of an Indian citizen/ Person of Indian Origin/ OCI cardholder (other than those who are registered as OCI cardholder) may be granted ‘X-2’
- Foreign nationals coming to join Auroville Foundation shall be issued X-3 visa.
- Foreign nationals coming to join the Missionaries of Charity, Kolkata may be granted multiple entry ‘X-3’ Visa.
- Foreign nationals who own property in India may be granted multiple entry ‘X-4’ Visa for a period of one year. The foreign national must submit documentary proof of permission of the Reserve Bank of India for the purchase of / holding the property in India and the document should show registration of the particular property with the registration authority concerned. The one year multi entry ‘X-4’ visa can be extended by the FRRO/FRO concerned on yearly basis up to a maximum period of 5 years from the date of initial visa.
- Diplomats/ Officials on a personal (Non-official) visit to India may be granted ‘X-5’ Visa for appropriate duration taking into account the purpose of visit.
- Ordinary passport holding dependents of diplomats/ officials assigned to India (including in the UN and Non-UN International Organizations) may be granted ‘X-5D’ Visa for appropriate duration.
- Participants in international Sports events and the officials (including coach) of the Sports teams (other than those who are engaged in commercial sports events in India on contract like Indian Premier League, Indian Soccer League, Indian Badminton League etc. with remuneration) may be granted ‘X-SP’ Visa of appropriate duration.
- Foreign nationals applying for visa for a purpose, which is not specifically covered under any visa category, may be granted ‘X-Misc’ Visa for appropriate duration.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

If the applicant had never held Indian passport, copy of last held Indian passport and surrender/deemed surrender certificate of parents or OCI/PIO card of parents along with birth certificate of applicant is required.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to transit  through India 
Transit visas are issued for transiting through India. It is valid for 15 days from the date of issue of the Visa. Applicant is permitted to stay for only 72 hrs in transit. Transit Visas can't be obtained from immigration counters at ports of entry in India. Confirmed onward travel to a destination outside India is required.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In accordance with the rules and regulations of the Government of India, the Embassy grants visa, on application, to those intending to transit  through India 
Transit visas are issued for transiting through India. It is valid for 15 days from the date of issue of the Visa. Applicant is permitted to stay for only 72 hrs in transit. Transit Visas can't be obtained from immigration counters at ports of entry in India. Confirmed onward travel to a destination outside India is required.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html).

visa_types.name: Tourist visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Copy of profile page of passport. If applicant is holding dual nationality, copy of profile page of both passports.
- Two recent Passport size photographs with white background
- Duly completed Online Application Form. The applicant must sign in the column under the photograph and on the last page.-
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Applicable Visa Fee
- Copy of Iraqi national ID. Non Iraqi passport holders need to provide a copy of their recent Iraq visa/visa extension page.
- Proof of confirmation of hotel accommodation in India
- Proof of availability of funds/bank statement for the duration of stay in India.
- Confirmed return tickets
- A letter from Employer/Sponsor or 3 months bank statement.
- In case of minors, documents from both  parents may be submitted
- Valid exit - entry copy for non-Iraqi
- Applicants who are not resident in Iraq are required to fill in the PERSONAL PARTICULARS FORM available at the end of these instructions in addition to the Visa application form.
- NOTE:
Tourist Visa is non-extendable, non-convertible and there should be a gap of 2 months between two successive visits to India.

The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Tourist visa is granted to a foreigner whose sole objective of visiting India is recreation, sight seeing, casual visit to meet friends or relatives and no other purpose/activity. 
NOTE:
- Tourist Visa shall be non-extendable and non-convertible to any other type of visa except in specific cases.
- Repeat visits there shall be a gap of at least 2 (two) months between two visits to India on a Tourist Visa.
- The applicant should furnish an undertaking that he/she is not engaged in Business activities, nor in Employment or pursuing studies/research etc. in India.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Tourist visa is granted to a foreigner whose sole objective of visiting India is recreation, sight seeing, casual visit to meet friends or relatives and no other purpose/activity. 
NOTE:
- Tourist Visa shall be non-extendable and non-convertible to any other type of visa except in specific cases.
- Repeat visits there shall be a gap of at least 2 (two) months between two visits to India on a Tourist Visa.

- Applicable Visa Fee
- Regular or Provisional admission letter in original from a recognized educational institution in India.
- A letter of financial support from parent / guardian along with a bank guarantee worth USD 5,000 (US Dollar Five Thousand) for the duration of the course/study.
- Father's employment letter indicating position held and salary attested by Chamber of Commerce/foreign Ministry.
- National ID copy
Applicants for admission in Medical or Para-medical courses are required to produce letter of approval or a " No Objection Certificate" from the Ministry of Health, Government of India.
Existing Students:
- Students who are already studying in India should produce the following documents along with their visa application:
- Bonafide student certificate from the college/university.
- Copy of residential permit/exit permit/Re-entry Visa issued by FRRO

Student Visa fees for Iraqi nationals:

[Table:    Student Visa]
Visa fee is processing charges. It cannot be refunded under any circumstances, whether Visa is denied or granted for a shorter period than applied for. Once Visa is granted, it cannot be changed or amended.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Student Visa is issued for pursuing regular and full time academic studies in India at educational institutions (Central/ State Government Educational Institutions & Private educational institutions) duly recognized by statutory regulatory body and have acquired statuary authorization to conduct the course(s).
- No other activities like business, employment, Journalism, etc., are permitted.
- It is normally issued for the duration of the course of study, subject to fulfilling all conditions.
- The applicant should submit proof of admission to a recognized educational institution in India along with documents certifying recognition of the Educational institution in India by concerned Government agency.
- Foreigners who intend to study yoga should apply for Student Visa. The Yoga institution should be recognised by Ministry of Home affairs, Govt. of India, for admitting foreign students.
- No Student visa will be issued to foreign nationals sponsored by franchise Educational institutes, Study Centres of Open Universities offering distance learning programmes, Educational institutes running “outreach” programmes and offering courses without any statutory sanction and regulatory authorities.
- Applicants must ensure Visa may take more than one month and therefore application for Visa must be submitted sufficiently well in advance before commencement of the course in India.
- Students are required to register with the nearest Foreigners Regional Registration Officer (FRRO) within 14 days of arrival in India.
- Students who are already studying in India should produce the following documents along with their visa application:
- Bonafide student certificate from the college/university.
- Copy of residential permit/exit permit/Re-entry Visa issued by FRRO
Applicants for admission in Medical or Para-medical courses are required to produce letter of approval or a " No Objection Certificate" from the Ministry of Health, Government of India.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Student Visa is issued for pursuing regular and full time academic studies in India at educational institutions (Central/ State Government Educational Institutions & Private educational institutions) duly recognized by statutory regulatory body and have acquired statuary authorization to conduct the course(s).
- No other activities like business, employment, Journalism, etc., are permitted.
- It is normally issued for the duration of the course of study, subject to fulfilling all conditions.
- The applicant should submit proof of admission to a recognized educational institution in India along with documents certifying recognition of the Educational institution in India by concerned Government agency.
- Foreigners who intend to study yoga should apply for Student Visa. The Yoga institution should be recognised by Ministry of Home affairs, Govt. of India, for admitting foreign students.
- No Student visa will be issued to foreign nationals sponsored by franchise Educational institutes, Study Centres of Open Universities offering distance learning programmes, Educational institutes running “outreach” programmes and offering courses without any statutory sanction and regulatory authorities.
- Applicants must ensure Visa may take more than one month and therefore application for Visa must be submitted sufficiently well in advance before commencement of the course in India.
- Students are required to register with the nearest Foreigners Regional Registration Officer (FRRO) within 14 days of arrival in India.
- Students who are already studying in India should produce the following documents along with their visa application:
- Bonafide student certificate from the college/university.
- Copy of residential permit/exit permit/Re-entry Visa issued by FRRO
Applicants for admission in Medical or Para-medical courses are required to produce letter of approval or a " No Objection Certificate" from the Ministry of Health, Government of India.

## Visa Fees
The fee depends upon the type applied for and its duration. Visa fee is divided into various types depending upon the case viz; basic fee, special fee & processing fee charged by VFS Global. During the online registration, at the end of the process the basic fee shall be displayed. However, this fee shall be indicative only and final fee shall be charged while submitting the application at the Visa application centre.
To know more about the indicative visa fees applicable to you based on your category and duration, please proceed to online registration (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/index.html). 
Student Visa fees for Iraqi nationals:

[Table:    Student Visa]
Visa fee is processing charges. It cannot be refunded under any circumstances, whether Visa is denied or granted for a shorter period than applied for. Once Visa is granted, it cannot be changed or amended.

## Documents Required
- Original Passport (valid for a minimum period of six months at the time of submission of visa application with two blank pages)
- Passport Copy
- Two recent Passport size photographs. Please See Photograph Requirements Document)
- Duly completed Online Application Form
https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/visa/indianVisaReg.jsp)
- Applicable Visa Fee
- Regular or Provisional admission letter in original from a recognized educational institution in India.
- A letter of financial support from parent / guardian along with a bank guarantee worth USD 5,000 (US Dollar Five Thousand) for the duration of the course/study.
- Father's employment letter indicating position held and salary attested by Chamber of Commerce/foreign Ministry.
- National ID copy
Applicants for admission in Medical or Para-medical courses are required to produce letter of approval or a " No Objection Certificate" from the Ministry of Health, Government of India.
Existing Students:
- Students who are already studying in India should produce the following documents along with their visa application:
- Bonafide student certificate from the college/university.
- Copy of residential permit/exit permit/Re-entry Visa issued by FRRO
